title: Topology Graph Infrastructure

module_descriptions.RGE_Field_Edge.py: Frozen dataclass RGEFieldEdge representing a directed connection between two topology nodes. Fields: source_node, target_node, weight (default 1.0), metadata. No traversal logic.
module_descriptions.RGE_Field_Graph.py: Class RGEFieldGraph providing dict-backed node storage and list-backed edge storage. Methods: add_node, add_edge, get_edges_from. Storage only — no pathfinding or routing.
module_descriptions.RGE_Graph_Builder.py: Class RGEGraphBuilder accepting a topology and a graph. connect_nodes verifies both node IDs exist in the topology before creating and registering an RGEFieldEdge. Raises ValueError on unknown node IDs.
module_descriptions.RGE_Graph_Traversal.py: Class RGEGraphTraversal providing read-only inspection. neighbors(node_id) returns the list of target_node values for all edges originating from node_id. No search algorithms or pathfinding.

design_decisions: RGEFieldEdge is frozen — immutable once constructed; weight defaults to 1.0 per spec.
RGEFieldGraph does not enforce uniqueness of edges; callers may add multiple edges between the same node pair (e.g., different weights).
RGEGraphBuilder.connect_nodes validates both node IDs against the topology before constructing the edge — fail-fast, no silent no-op.
RGEGraphTraversal.neighbors returns an empty list for unknown node IDs rather than raising — safe read-only probe.
No routing, pathfinding, resonance, or decay logic introduced in any module.
